changes_summary: Addressed both significant flags. FLAG-001: drain effect no longer dequeues before sending — it awaits `sendNow` and only slices the head on success; `sendNow`'s `catch` clears `optimisticMessage` so the drain effect isn't permanently blocked; a failed head stays in the queue so the user can delete/reorder rather than silently losing it or retry-looping. FLAG-002: `optimisticMessage` gains `sentAtMs` and `priorTurnCount` fields, and the clear effect now requires a new user turn beyond that count with matching content and a sufficiently recent timestamp, so duplicate-text messages no longer prematurely satisfy the match. Added matching regression tests. No scope growth — all changes remain inside `AgentChat.tsx` plus its test file.
questions: Should a failed head item be auto-retried after the 5s `localError` auto-clear, or remain paused until user action? Current plan: paused until user action (safer, matches minimal scope).
Should attachments captured at queue time be visible as thumbnails on the queued card, or is a count badge sufficient? Default: count badge.
Should the queue persist if the user closes the chat panel (`isOpen=false`)? Default: yes — local React state in a still-mounted component.
Is `AgentTurn.timestamp` numeric ms or an ISO string? Plan assumes numeric ms; if ISO, use `Date.parse` in the optimistic-clear comparator.

success_criteria.criterion: While the agent is processing a message, the input field accepts typing and the Send button can be pressed.
Submitting while busy adds a card to a queued-messages stack above the input rather than calling the send mutation.
Each queued card shows the message text, a delete control, and up/down reorder controls (up disabled at top, down disabled at bottom).
When the active send finishes (mutation resolves AND `isProcessing` is false AND the hardened optimistic-clear effect has fired), the head of the queue is removed and auto-sent.
Each queued message sends with the attachments captured at queue time, not current clip selection.

Two queued items with identical text do not cause premature draining — the optimistic-clear effect matches only the new user turn produced by the current send (turn count grew beyond `priorTurnCount` and timestamp >= `sentAtMs`).
A failed queued send keeps its item at the head of the queue, surfaces the existing error banner, and pauses further draining until the user deletes the failed item.
Cancelling the session and creating a new session both clear the queue and any pending optimistic state.
Diff is confined to `AgentChat.tsx` and `AgentChat.test.tsx`; no hook/server/DB changes.
Existing AgentChat tests still pass; new tests cover queueing, drain-on-completion, duplicate-text regression, failed-send regression, and reorder/delete.
`AgentChat.tsx` stays under ~900 LOC and the queue logic is contained, not scattered.
Manual smoke: queue 3 messages (including one duplicate-text) with different clip selections; they process in order with correct attachments; a forced error pauses draining.

assumptions: Local client-side queue only; no persistence across reloads.
`useSendMessage` rejects or no-ops if called while a turn is in flight, so `isProcessing` must flip false before draining.
After the hardening in Step 2, the optimistic-clear effect reliably signals the previous send's user turn is in history (matches on turn-count growth, new timestamp, and content — not content alone).
On a failed queued send, the safest default is to pause draining and keep the failed item at the head for the user to delete/reorder. Auto-retry is deliberately NOT implemented.
Cancel and New-session discard pending queued items; session switch does too.
Voice-transcribed messages flow through `handleSend` and therefore queue when busy.
`ChevronUp`/`ChevronDown` from `lucide-react` are available (already used elsewhere in the repo).
`AgentTurn.timestamp` is numeric milliseconds suitable for comparison with `Date.now()`; if it's an ISO string, convert via `Date.parse` at compare-time.
Tests use vitest; patterns derivable from existing `AgentChat.test.tsx`.
structure_warnings: Each step section should reference at least one file in backticks.
